title: Elizabeth Holmes verdict, fraud origins & takeaways, navigating "The Great Markdown" & more
description:
0:00 Bestie intro and poker recap

3:14 Elizabeth Holmes verdict, origins of the fraud, breakdown of the Theranos promise

17:51 Lessons for founders and investors from the Theranos debacle, bad behavior a sign of the top

26:09 Implications of "The Great Markdown" on public and private markets, confusing FED decisions

45:56 Advice for founders on navigating a downturn

1:00:01 a16z's new mega funds; should they go public? How does that change partner dynamics?

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses the Elizabeth Holmes verdict, the origins of the Theranos fraud, and lessons for founders and investors. They also navigate the implications of 'The Great Markdown' on public and private markets and provide advice for founders on navigating a downturn. Additionally, they talk about a16z's new mega funds and whether they should go public.

takeaways.text: Bad behavior can be a sign of the top
Founders should focus on unit economics
Investors should be cautious of confusing FED decisions
Theranos debacle provides lessons for founders and investors
a16z's new mega funds raise questions about going public
Partner dynamics may change if a16z goes public
Founders should prioritize transparency and accountability

quotes.text: Bad behavior is a sign of the top
quotes.speaker: Chamath Palihapitiya

sentiment.notes: The hosts discuss the implications of the Theranos verdict and the current market conditions, advising founders and investors to be cautious and prepared for a potential downturn.

notable_moments.description: Discussion of the Elizabeth Holmes verdict and Theranos fraud
Lessons for founders and investors from the Theranos debacle
Advice for founders on navigating a downturn
Discussion of a16z's new mega funds and potential implications of going public
